posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
A couple of very nice bottles on a low-key NYE.

Happy New Year to all.

2002 Krug: Super complex and engaging nose with whiffs of smoke, ginger, aniseed, preserved citrus, grilled nuts and bread. It is explosive in the mouth, with the usual Krug power and a core of sublime fruit. It builds through the palate finishing with superb cut. The finish is all lemon and mineral and length is phenomenal.

1969 Maison Leroy Nuits-St-Georges 1er Cru: Fully mature and somewhere near its apogee. Highly perfumed nose of strawberries, damp earth, smoked meats and decaying leaves. It is full, sweet and vinous in the mouth with a sensual silky feel against the gums. It is a delicate wine but possessing good depth and persistence. A joy to drink.

posts.name: Ken Strauss
posts.cooked:
Jeremy Holmes:

A couple of very nice bottles on a low-key NYE.

Happy New Year to all.

2002 Krug: Super complex and engaging nose with whiffs of smoke, ginger, aniseed, preserved citrus, grilled nuts and bread. It is explosive in the mouth, with the usual Krug power and a core of sublime fruit. It builds through the palate finishing with superb cut. The finish is all lemon and mineral and length is phenomenal.

1969 Maison Leroy Nuits-St-Georges 1er Cru: Fully mature and somewhere near its apogee. Highly perfumed nose of strawberries, damp earth, smoked meats and decaying leaves. It is full, sweet and vinous in the mouth with a sensual silky feel against the gums. It is a delicate wine but possessing good depth and persistence. A joy to drink.

Thanks for notes!
Especially the Krug.
Now I know what to open at midnight.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Nice notes. The first great red Burgundy I ever tasted was a 1969. Always had a soft spot for the vintage.

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked: yes, Happy New Year. Will the new World see you this year?

posts.name: Laurent Gibet
posts.cooked:
Jeremy Holmes:

A couple of very nice bottles on a low-key NYE.

Happy New Year to all.

2002 Krug: Super complex and engaging nose with whiffs of smoke, ginger, aniseed, preserved citrus, grilled nuts and bread. It is explosive in the mouth, with the usual Krug power and a core of sublime fruit. It builds through the palate finishing with superb cut. The finish is all lemon and mineral and length is phenomenal.

1969 Maison Leroy Nuits-St-Georges 1er Cru: Fully mature and somewhere near its apogee. Highly perfumed nose of strawberries, damp earth, smoked meats and decaying leaves. It is full, sweet and vinous in the mouth with a sensual silky feel against the gums. It is a delicate wine but possessing good depth and persistence. A joy to drink.

Happy 2018 …

I finished mine with friends and we had several great wines :
Champagne Pol Roger Winston Churchill 1993
Jurançon Clos Joliette 1971 (dry and one of the youngest 1971 I ever had)

Rousseau Clos St-Jacques 2008 (starting its life)
Angerville Volnay Clos des Ducs 1999 (idem)

posts.name: Blake Brown
posts.cooked: Nice, eloquent notes Jeremy. Also a nice combination, Krug and Leroy. The best in the new year to you and yours.

posts.name: Don Cornutt
posts.cooked:
2002 Krug is monumental. I am a buyer every opportunity at a reasonable rate.
It will be priceless in a few years IMHO. Like the 1988 it will skyrocket.

posts.name: Daniel Bailey
posts.cooked:
dcornutt:

2002 Krug is monumental. I am a buyer every opportunity at a reasonable rate.
It will be priceless in a few years IMHO. Like the 1988 it will skyrocket.

Agree 100%. I loaded up early on with everything I could get my hands on.

posts.name: Tom Gutting
posts.cooked: Nice notes, Jeremy. The 2002 Krug is a special wine. Had a bottle at L’Atelier Robuchon in Vegas last year, and it was a superstar with the first half of that menu. Truly great. The Leroy sounds like a treat.

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
Hi Alan and Jerry,

I don’t reckon we’ll be in The States this year but I promise we’ll visit you in 2019.

Cheers
Jeremy

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
Jeremy Holmes:

Hi Alan and Jerry,

I don’t reckon we’ll be in The States this year but I promise we’ll visit you in 2019.

Cheers
Jeremy

that’ll give the wines another year to age!

posts.name: Adam P
posts.cooked: Also drank some 02 Krug on New Year’s Eve. Followed by an 04 Perrier Jouet belle epoque, a 14 SQN Syrah, and then a Krug 164e at midnight. All fantastic. I have a special place in my heart for Krug. I picked up a 6 pack of the 02 and two 6 packs of the 164e for what I thought was a steal a couple months back
